prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arr matey, ye be askin' why the sky be blue? Well, I'll tell ye a tale. Ye see, the sun be shinin' its light down upon us, and that light be made up of all sorts o' colors, like a rainbow. But our eyes, they be more keen to the blue part o' that light, on account o' the way the air be scatterin' it about. So when ye look up, all ye see be that blue, like the deep sea on a clear day. That be why the sky be blue, me hearty!
